refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable measurement and reporting of energy consumption per unit of work output.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates two distinct obligations: (1) the ability to measure/report energy efficiency metrics, and (2) the achievement of a target value. The latter is a performance constraint on the system's operation, not a functional requirement of the system itself. The refined FR focuses on the functional capability—providing visibility into energy efficiency—which is solution-neutral and measurable. Achievement of the energy_per_ton ≤ E_target acceptance criterion is a system-level performance requirement that emerges from correct design of propulsion, hydraulics, and operational control DPs, not from a single FR.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate that the NGHE solution can capture energy_used (kWh or equivalent) and work_output (tons moved) over a defined evaluation period, compute energy_per_ton, and report the result to authorized stakeholders (Site Manager, Maintenance). Verify data logging, calculation accuracy, and accessibility of the metric.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall acquire and log energy consumption data during operation.
FR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall acquire and log work output (mass moved) data during operation.
FR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall compute energy efficiency (energy per unit work output) from logged data.
FR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall provide authorized stakeholders access to energy efficiency metrics and historical trends.

candidate_dps: DP1: Instrument energy input (fuel/electricity) at source; instrument work output (tonnage moved) at point of delivery; compute ratio offline post-evaluation period
DP2: Deploy real-time energy and tonnage sensors with continuous data aggregation; compute energy_per_ton at configurable intervals; expose metric via reporting interface

DP3: Integrate energy metering into power distribution subsystem; integrate tonnage measurement into load/transport subsystem; synchronize timestamps and compute ratio in a dedicated metering logic module
DP4: Establish energy accounting via fuel consumption logs and work logs (manual or semi-automated); reconcile and compute energy_per_ton through periodic audit process

DP5: Decompose into two independent measurement streams—(a) energy consumption measurement with configurable granularity, (b) work output quantification with independent sensor/data path—unified only at reporting layer via time-windowed aggregation
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP5: Decompose into two independent measurement streams—(a) energy consumption measurement with configurable granularity, (b) work output quantification with independent sensor/data path—unified only at reporting layer via time-windowed aggregation

potential_sources: Timestamp synchronization between energy and tonnage streams (mitigated by loose coupling at reporting layer)
Shared data infrastructure (acceptable if interfaces are well-defined)
Dependency on common time reference (acceptable; standard practice)
coupling_check.notes: DP5 achieves low coupling by keeping energy measurement and work output measurement logically independent until the reporting/aggregation layer. This allows each stream to evolve (e.g., sensor type, sampling rate, calibration) without forcing changes to the other. DP1 is too coarse (post-hoc only, poor feedback). DP2 risks tight coupling between sensor acquisition and computation. DP3 couples metering logic to specific subsystems. DP4 introduces manual process risk and poor real-time visibility. DP5 balances independence with unified reporting.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Time synchronization across energy and tonnage measurement streams is achievable within acceptable tolerance (±5% of evaluation window recommended). Risk: Clock drift or network latency could skew ratio. Mitigation: Establish time-sync protocol and validation checks.

Assumption: Energy source is singular or aggregatable (e.g., single fuel tank, single power feed). Risk: Multi-source energy (e.g., hybrid power) complicates accounting. Mitigation: Design energy measurement to support multiple input streams if needed.

Assumption: Work output (tonnage) is measurable at a single point or aggregatable from multiple points. Risk: Distributed work (e.g., multiple load points) may require distributed sensors. Mitigation: Define work output measurement boundary clearly in IM layer.
Assumption: Evaluation period is fixed and known in advance. Risk: Partial-period or rolling-window reporting may be needed. Mitigation: Ensure reporting layer supports configurable time windows.

Assumption: Acceptance criterion E_target is stable over evaluation period. Risk: Target may need adjustment based on operational conditions. Mitigation: Document target derivation and review process.
Assumption: Data loss or sensor failure is acceptable within defined limits. Risk: Missing data could invalidate ratio. Mitigation: Define data quality thresholds and reporting caveats.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: FR (refined): The NGHE solution shall enable continuous measurement of energy consumption and work output (tonnage moved) via independent, loosely-coupled measurement streams, and shall compute and report the ratio energy_per_ton = energy_used / ton_moved at configurable intervals, such that the reported metric satisfies energy_per_ton ≤ E_target over the agreed evaluation period, with documented data quality and time-sync assumptions. DP5 supports this by isolating measurement concerns and deferring unification to the reporting layer, reducing architectural coupling and enabling independent evolution of energy and work-output subsystems.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Unified measurement and reporting system encompassing dual independent measurement streams (energy consumption and work output) with time-windowed aggregation at reporting layer

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exports aggregated energy and work output metrics via time-windowed reports to external monitoring/logging systems

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task directives and granularity configuration parameters for energy measurement stream

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Transmits telemetry reports and receives configuration updates for measurement parameters

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent containment: NGHE Solution is the natural home for measurement orchestration, dual-stream independence, and unified reporting. Energy consumption measurement and work output quantification are core NGHE functions. Time-windowed aggregation logic resides entirely within this boundary. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry export and task configuration ingress cross boundary.

Poor containment: Vehicle module would be responsible for physical actuation and basic sensor data collection, but DP5 requires independent measurement stream logic, aggregation algorithms, and reporting orchestration—all of which are NGHE-level concerns. Significant diffusion: energy measurement granularity configuration, work output quantification logic, and time-windowed aggregation would leak into NGHE Solution anyway.

Very poor containment: Teleoperation is a command/control interface layer, not a measurement system. Placing dual measurement streams and aggregation logic here violates separation of concerns. Nearly all measurement and reporting logic would diffuse to NGHE Solution, creating architectural incoherence.

Very poor containment: Safety Services is responsible for hazard detection and safety constraints, not measurement orchestration. Energy and work output measurement streams are operational metrics, not safety-critical signals. Placing DP5 here would create inappropriate coupling and diffuse core measurement logic elsewhere.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: DP5 (dual independent measurement streams with unified time-windowed reporting) is fundamentally a measurement orchestration and data fusion concern that belongs at the NGHE Solution level. The two independent streams (energy consumption with configurable granularity and work output quantification) are independent data acquisition paths that converge only at the reporting layer—a pattern that naturally fits within a unified measurement subsystem owned by NGHE Solution. External interfaces (Telemetry for export, Tasking for configuration, Network Link for bidirectional communication) provide clean boundaries without requiring measurement logic to diffuse into Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services.
containment_risks: Risk: If energy consumption measurement requires real-time feedback to Vehicle control loops, tight coupling may emerge between NGHE Solution measurement stream and Vehicle actuation. Mitigation: Ensure energy measurement is observational only; any control feedback must flow through explicit Tasking interface.

Risk: If work output quantification depends on Vehicle-specific sensor calibration or hardware characteristics, sensor abstraction may leak into NGHE Solution. Mitigation: Define a clean sensor data contract (via Network Link or internal interface) that Vehicle provides without NGHE Solution needing to know hardware details.

Risk: Time-windowed aggregation logic may require synchronization with external systems (e.g., cloud telemetry backends). If synchronization becomes complex, reporting layer may diffuse into multiple modules. Mitigation: Encapsulate all aggregation and windowing logic within NGHE Solution; expose only final reports via Telemetry interface.

Risk: Configurable granularity for energy measurement may require dynamic reconfiguration during operation. If configuration state becomes distributed, coherence may suffer. Mitigation: Centralize all configuration state within NGHE Solution; Tasking interface provides single point of configuration ingress.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP5 directly addresses the FR by decomposing energy consumption measurement and work output quantification into two independent streams unified at the reporting layer. This design enables the required 'energy per unit of work output' metric (energy_per_ton = energy_used / ton_moved) through time-windowed aggregation. The configurable granularity for energy measurement provides flexibility to meet evaluation period requirements. DP5 is logically sound and traceable to the FR.

diffusion_risks: Energy measurement stream may require real-time feedback coupling to Vehicle control loops if energy optimization becomes a closed-loop concern. Current design assumes observational-only measurement; if Vehicle actuation must respond to energy metrics, tight coupling risk emerges. Mitigation: Enforce unidirectional data flow from Vehicle sensors to NGHE measurement stream; any control feedback must route through explicit Tasking interface.

Work output quantification depends on sensor data contracts (e.g., distance traveled, load mass, work unit definitions). If sensor calibration or hardware-specific interpretation logic leaks into NGHE Solution, abstraction boundary weakens. Mitigation: Define a formal sensor data schema via Network Link or internal interface; Vehicle provides calibrated sensor values without NGHE needing hardware knowledge.

Time-windowed aggregation logic may require clock synchronization with external telemetry backends or distributed systems. If synchronization state becomes distributed across NGHE Solution and external systems, coherence and auditability suffer. Mitigation: Encapsulate all windowing, aggregation, and timestamp logic within NGHE Solution; expose only finalized reports via Telemetry interface.

Configurable granularity for energy measurement (e.g., per-task, per-minute, per-ton) may require dynamic reconfiguration during operation. If configuration state is replicated or cached across modules, consistency risks emerge. Mitigation: Centralize configuration state within NGHE Solution; Tasking interface is the single authoritative ingress point.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution achieves 0.92 containment score with 0.08 diffusion. The dual-stream independence pattern (energy consumption measurement and work output quantification) naturally converges at the reporting layer within a single measurement orchestration module. External interfaces (Telemetry export, Tasking configuration ingress, Network Link bidirectional communication) provide clean architectural boundaries. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules show poor containment (0.35, 0.15, 0.2 respectively) with high diffusion (0.65, 0.85, 0.8), confirming that NGHE Solution is the correct ownership boundary. No measurement logic should leak into Vehicle actuation, Teleoperation command/control, or Safety Services hazard detection.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Acceptance criterion 'energy_per_ton ≤ E_target over evaluation period' is directly verifiable through NGHE Solution's time-windowed aggregation output. Energy consumption measurement stream provides numerator (energy_used); work output quantification stream provides denominator (ton_moved). Telemetry interface exports aggregated metrics for external validation. Verification requires: (1) energy measurement calibration and granularity configuration documented, (2) work output sensor accuracy and unit definitions specified, (3) time-window boundaries and aggregation algorithm formally defined, (4) evaluation period start/end timestamps recorded. All verification data flows through NGHE Solution's reporting layer, enabling traceability.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns measurement orchestration, dual-stream independence, and unified reporting. Tasking interface provides configuration ingress (granularity settings, evaluation period parameters). Network Link provides sensor data ingress and telemetry export. Vehicle provides raw sensor data (energy consumption signals, work output indicators) without measurement logic. Worksite safety plan and operational policies are external constraints that inform task directives and granularity configuration but do not alter NGHE Solution's measurement responsibility. No ambiguity in module ownership or interface contracts.

recommended_revisions: Formalize sensor data contract: Define a schema for energy consumption signals (units, sampling rate, calibration metadata) and work output indicators (distance, load, unit definitions) that Vehicle provides to NGHE Solution. This prevents abstraction leakage and enables independent testing of measurement streams.

Specify time-window aggregation algorithm: Document the exact windowing strategy (tumbling, sliding, session-based), timestamp semantics (event time vs. processing time), and handling of out-of-order or late-arriving data. This ensures reproducible energy_per_ton calculations and supports auditability for acceptance criterion verification.

Define configuration state management: Establish a formal configuration schema for energy measurement granularity (per-task, per-minute, per-ton, etc.) with versioning and change-log tracking. Ensure Tasking interface is the sole authoritative source; NGHE Solution must not accept configuration from other modules.

Establish measurement stream independence verification: Add a design review checkpoint to confirm that energy consumption measurement and work output quantification have no shared state, no cross-stream dependencies, and no implicit ordering assumptions. This validates the independence claim in DP5.

Document telemetry export format and cadence: Specify the structure of exported energy_per_ton reports (timestamp, window boundaries, energy value, work output value, calculated ratio, confidence/quality metrics). Define export frequency and retry/buffering behavior to ensure external monitoring systems can reliably consume metrics.

Add observational-only constraint to energy measurement: Explicitly document that energy measurement is read-only with respect to Vehicle control loops. If future requirements demand energy-aware actuation, this must be handled through a separate control loop, not by embedding feedback logic in the measurement stream.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass with high confidence, and the design is logically sound. However, the recommended_revisions list identifies six critical formalization gaps that must be resolved before proceeding to sub-level decomposition. Given the high safety criticality of this system, these gaps represent unacceptable risk: (1) sensor data contract formalization is essential to prevent abstraction leakage into Vehicle module, (2) time-window aggregation algorithm specification is mandatory for reproducible acceptance criterion verification, (3) configuration state management formalization prevents consistency failures in distributed operation, (4) measurement stream independence verification is a design review checkpoint that must pass before architectural commitment, (5) telemetry export format specification ensures external monitoring reliability, and (6) observational-only constraint documentation prevents future coupling risks. Proceeding to sub-level decomposition without these formalizations would create technical debt in the measurement orchestration layer and compromise auditability for safety-critical energy efficiency evaluation. REVISE_CURRENT_LEVEL allows DP5 to mature to production-ready specification before sub-decomposition.

revise.why: DP5 is logically correct but insufficiently specified for implementation and verification. The revision must formalize: (1) sensor data contract schema (energy consumption signal units, sampling rate, calibration metadata; work output indicator definitions for distance, load, unit semantics), (2) time-window aggregation algorithm with explicit windowing strategy (tumbling vs. sliding vs. session-based), timestamp semantics (event-time vs. processing-time), and late-data handling, (3) configuration state management schema with versioning and change-log tracking, enforcing Tasking interface as sole authoritative ingress, (4) measurement stream independence constraints (no shared state, no cross-stream dependencies, no implicit ordering), (5) telemetry export format specification (timestamp, window boundaries, energy value, work output value, calculated ratio, confidence metrics, export frequency, retry/buffering), and (6) explicit observational-only constraint on energy measurement with prohibition on feedback coupling to Vehicle control loops. These formalizations transform DP5 from a conceptual design into a specification-ready design parameter suitable for architectural commitment and sub-level decomposition.

assumptions: Energy measurement stream operates in observational-only mode with no feedback coupling to Vehicle actuation control loops.
Work output quantification depends on calibrated sensor data provided by Vehicle module without measurement logic embedded in Vehicle.
Time-windowed aggregation is performed entirely within NGHE Solution with no distributed state or external clock synchronization dependencies.
Configuration state (granularity, evaluation period parameters) is centralized within NGHE Solution with Tasking interface as sole authoritative ingress point.
Sensor data contracts (energy consumption signals, work output indicators) are formally defined and enforced at Network Link interface boundary.
Telemetry export is unidirectional from NGHE Solution to external monitoring systems with no feedback control loop.
Energy per unit work output metric (energy_per_ton) is calculated deterministically within NGHE Solution's reporting layer with full auditability.
open_risks: Sensor data contract leakage: If Vehicle module embeds measurement interpretation logic (e.g., energy unit conversion, work output calculation) instead of providing raw calibrated signals, abstraction boundary weakens and NGHE Solution loses measurement ownership. Mitigation: Formalize sensor data schema and enforce via interface contract validation.

Time-window coherence in distributed operation: If NGHE Solution is deployed across multiple instances or if external telemetry backends perform secondary aggregation, timestamp semantics and window boundary alignment may diverge, causing non-reproducible energy_per_ton calculations. Mitigation: Encapsulate all windowing logic within single NGHE Solution instance; export only finalized reports.

Configuration consistency under dynamic reconfiguration: If granularity settings or evaluation period parameters are changed during operation without version tracking or change-log, historical energy_per_ton calculations may become non-auditable. Mitigation: Implement configuration versioning with immutable change-log; tag all reports with configuration version.

Measurement stream independence violation: If energy consumption measurement and work output quantification share state (e.g., common timestamp buffer, shared sensor data cache), independence assumption fails and cross-stream dependencies may introduce subtle ordering bugs. Mitigation: Enforce architectural separation via code review checkpoint; use separate data structures for each stream.

Observational-only constraint erosion: If future requirements demand energy-aware vehicle actuation (e.g., load balancing, efficiency optimization), pressure may emerge to embed feedback logic in energy measurement stream, violating observational-only design. Mitigation: Document constraint explicitly; route any future control feedback through separate control loop, not through measurement stream.

Telemetry export reliability: If external monitoring systems cannot reliably consume energy_per_ton reports due to format ambiguity, missing metadata, or inconsistent export cadence, acceptance criterion verification becomes unreliable. Mitigation: Formalize export format with confidence/quality metrics; implement retry/buffering logic.
evidence_needed: Formal sensor data contract schema document specifying energy consumption signal units (kWh, J, etc.), sampling rate, calibration metadata structure, and work output indicator definitions (distance units, load mass units, work unit semantics).

Time-window aggregation algorithm specification document with explicit windowing strategy (tumbling, sliding, session-based), timestamp semantics (event-time vs. processing-time), handling of out-of-order/late data, and edge-case behavior (window boundary alignment, partial windows).
Configuration state management schema with versioning strategy, change-log structure, and enforcement mechanism ensuring Tasking interface is sole authoritative ingress point.

Design review checkpoint evidence confirming measurement stream independence: no shared state, no cross-stream dependencies, no implicit ordering assumptions between energy consumption measurement and work output quantification streams.

Telemetry export format specification document with JSON/protobuf schema, metadata fields (timestamp, window boundaries, energy value, work output value, calculated ratio, confidence/quality metrics), export frequency, and retry/buffering behavior.
Observational-only constraint documentation with explicit prohibition on feedback coupling to Vehicle control loops and guidance for future control loop requirements.

Acceptance criterion verification procedure document detailing how energy_per_ton ≤ E_target is measured, recorded, and audited over evaluation period, including calibration validation, sensor accuracy requirements, and timestamp traceability.
